blurb: Justification by faith ushers us into a peace that the law could never provide—not the fragile truce of perfect compliance, but the settled rest of those who have been declared righteous apart from works. This peace with the Father flows from grace, not achievement, creating a foundation so secure that even sufferings become instruments of transformation rather than threats to our standing. Where the Demiurge's system breeds anxiety over performance, the Stranger God's mercy produces a confidence that can actually rejoice in trials, knowing that nothing can separate us from the love revealed in Christ.

verses.text: What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound?
God forbid. How shall we, that are dead to sin, live any longer therein?
Know ye not, that so many of us as were baptized into Jesus Christ were baptized into his death?
Therefore we are buried with him by baptism into death: that like as Christ was raised up from the dead by the glory of the Father, even so we also should walk in newness of life.
